data.overview: Before finalising any extraction, run plausibility checks to catch scale errors, double-counting, and omissions. These are the checks an experienced estimator applies instinctively.

building_footprint_checks.rule: Compare extracted quantities against the building's physical dimensions. If quantities don't make sense for the building size, something is wrong.

checks.check: Main duct run vs building length
checks.rule_of_thumb: Main supply/return duct on one floor should be 1–3× the building's longest dimension
checks.example: 50m building → main supply duct should be ~50–150m on that floor. If you get 500m, suspect scale error (2×) or wrong drawing.
checks.if_fails: Re-check scale. Cross-validate against a known dimension on the drawing.

checks.rule_of_thumb: Total LTHW pipework per floor is typically 2–5m per radiator/FCU served (branches + mains share)
checks.example: Floor has 20 FCUs → expect 40–100m of total pipework on that floor. If you get 500m, check for double-counting or scale error.
checks.if_fails: Review measurement method. Are you measuring the same pipe runs twice from overlapping drawings?

checks.rule_of_thumb: FCUs/cassettes: typically 1 per 15–30m² in offices. Radiators: 1 per room or 1 per 3–5m of external wall.
checks.example: 500m² office floor → expect 17–33 FCUs. If you get 5, you're missing some. If you get 100, you're double-counting.
checks.if_fails: Cross-check against equipment schedule if available.

checks.rule_of_thumb: Each supply diffuser serves ~10–20m² floor area. Branch run to diffuser: typically 2–8m.
checks.example: 12 diffusers with 2–8m branches each → 24–96m of branch ductwork. If branch total is 400m, something is wrong.
checks.if_fails: Check individual branch measurements. Verify scale.

checks.rule_of_thumb: Cable tray/basket: typically 0.3–0.8m per m² of floor area (offices). Data-heavy buildings higher.
checks.example: 500m² floor → 150–400m of containment. If you get 2000m, suspect double-counting.
checks.if_fails: Are you counting containment on both ceiling plan and services coordination drawing? Measure from one source only.

system_completeness_checks.rule: For each system identified, verify the extraction is logically complete — every system has a source, distribution, and terminal.

must_have: Isolation valves at plant and branches
common_omissions: Return pipework (often forgotten — should equal flow meterage)
Isolation valves (always needed but often not drawn)
Insulation with vapour barrier (essential for CHW to prevent condensation)
Fire dampers (life-safety — must be at every rated wall/floor penetration)
Access doors (required per DW/144 at regular intervals)
Submain cables from main switchboard to DBs
Earthing (not always shown on lighting/power layouts)
Fire-rated cables for fire alarm and emergency lighting

quantity_ratio_checks.rule: Certain quantities should maintain expected ratios. If ratios are wildly off, investigate.

ratios.if_fails: Check if return is on a different drawing or measured from a different source.

ratios.tolerance: Return is typically 70–100% of supply (some areas may be transfer air)
ratios.if_fails: Check if extract system is on a separate drawing or different discipline.

ratios.ratio: Number of fire dampers ≈ Number of fire-rated wall/floor penetrations by duct
ratios.if_fails: If fire dampers < penetrations, flag as [FIRE DAMPERS POTENTIALLY MISSING].

qa_process.rule: Before releasing extraction output, perform these quality checks.
checklist: Every item has a Source label (Schedule/Drawing/Scale/Estimated/Implicit)
Every item has a Confidence score (High/Medium/Low)
Low confidence items have a specific flag explaining why
Scale has been cross-validated against a known reference dimension
Drawing revision is the latest available
No linear quantities measured from schematics or riser diagrams
All systems have both source and return/extract measured
Equipment counts match schedule where schedule exists
Fire dampers counted at every fire-rated penetration
Quantities pass plausibility checks against building footprint
